active_alerts.description: Please see station personnel or use a station call box to request assistance.

Skipped outbound stops:

Skipped inbound stops:
Ruggles

All buses serving Ruggles Station are temporarily not using Ruggles Upper Busway. All drop‑offs will occur only at Ruggles Lower.

This is to accommodate track improvement work and the replacement of 14,000 railroad ties and is scheduled to complete mid-May, 2026.

Single-track operations will be in effect throughout this period so boarding areas may change.

Temporarily Suspended Inbound Trains:
•	126 (8:49am from Newburyport)
•	1132 (10:53am from Beverly)
•	46 (1:47pm from Rockport)
•	1150 (3:23 from Beverly)

Temporarily Suspended Outbound Trains:
•	125 (8:35am to Newburyport)
•	1129 (9:35am to Beverly)
•	39 (12:05pm to Rockport)
•	11:47am (2:05pm to Beverly)
Please see station personnel or use a station call box to request assistance.

There are accessible mini-high platforms at the Western end of both the Track 1 and Track 2 sides. However, the existing pedestrian path between the two sides is long and includes steep slopes, especially on the Crest Road bridge over the tracks. Designs are underway to provide for a fully compliant pathway.

All buses serving Ruggles Station are temporarily not using Ruggles Upper Busway. All drop‑offs will occur only at Ruggles Lower.

Skipped outbound stops:
Ruggles

Skipped outbound stops:
Ruggles

All buses serving Ruggles Station are temporarily not using Ruggles Upper Busway. All drop‑offs will occur only at Ruggles Lower.

Accessibility:
Shuttle bus service is not available at Saint Mary’s Street, Kent Street, or Brandon Hall in both directions. Riders should instead utilize shuttle bus service at the next nearest stop.

Alternate Service:
Riders are also encouraged to utilize nearby D Branch service at Reservoir.

Peak Period Limited Shuttle Service:
A limited stop shuttle will be offered during peak hours, serving Cleveland Circle, Washington Sq, Coolidge Corner, and Kenmore only.

Walking Alternatives:
Passengers can also walk from Cleveland Circle to Reservoir Station (3-4 min) or from Dean Road to Beaconsfield (4-5 min), for D Branch service.

Travel Time:
Riders using shuttles bus service should budget ample extra travel time. For example, a rider travelling to Government Center from Cleveland Circle should budget at least an additional 15 minutes of travel in additional to their regular commute.

Additional Stop Closures:

Outbound Green Line trains will not stop at Englewood Avenue from May 18 to July 10:
 
Trains will bypass the stop during this time period. Riders can instead board or disembark one stop away at either Cleveland Circle or Dean Road, which are 0.2 miles away (or a 4- to 5-minute walk).
 
Outbound Green Line trains will not stop at Tappan Street from May 18 to July 10:
 
Trains will bypass the stop during this time period. Riders can instead board or disembark one stop away at either Washington Square or Dean Road, which are 0.2 miles away (or a 3- to 4-minute walk).
 
Inbound Green Line trains will not stop at Tappan Street from May 18 to June 12:

Riders can instead board or disembark one stop away at either Washington Square or Dean Road.

To exit, exit the train at Harvard, then take an Ashmont or Braintree-bound train to Central and use Central Elevator 861. To board, cross Mass Ave to the Route 1 bus stop at Mass Ave and Prospect St, and take a Route 1 (Harvard) bus to Harvard to access the Red Line.
Scheduled to complete sometime in 2027, the Reconstruction of Foster Street includes widening and resurfacing with the addition of bicycle lanes.

To board, travel along the busway to the Mattapan Line/busway lobby and use Ashmont Elevator 969.

Accessibility:
Free and accessible shuttle buses will replace service between Ashmont and Mattapan.

Alternative service:
Riders can also use the Fairmount Commuter Rail Line at Blue Hill Ave Station.  

Travel Time:
Riders using shuttles bus service should budget ample extra travel time. For example, a rider travelling to Ashmont from Mattapan should budget at least an additional 15 minutes of travel in additional to their regular commute.

Northern Ave @ Tide St

Skipped inbound stops:
Northern Ave @ Tide St
Please use nearby ramp to the platform.

Affected lines:
Fall River/New Bedford Line

Braintree branch riders:
Regular service will operate between JFK/UMass and Braintree, but riders must take a shuttle bus between Broadway and JFK/UMass.

Accessibility:
Free and accessible shuttle buses will operate between Broadway and Ashmont. Accessible van service will be available between Broadway and Ashmont. Riders should see station personnel for assistance.

Travel times:
Riders using shuttles bus service should budget ample extra travel time. For example, a rider travelling to Park Street from Ashmont should budget at least an additional 30 – 40 minutes of travel in additional to their regular commute.

Alternate service:
Riders are encouraged to consider Commuter Rail service between South Station and Braintree, which will be free during this service change. Riders arriving and departing from South Station should see Keolis staff for free access through the fare gates. Regular fares should be purchased for travel beyond Braintree.

The escalator will remain out of service for the foreseeable future. This decision was made after a thorough inspection determined that corrosion on the escalator’s steel truss was so severe that it could not be repaired further and that a complete replacement of the escalator would be required. Replacing the escalator at this location requires a more extended unit meeting modern escalator safety requirements. This longer unit will require significant alteration to the station entrance to meet code requirements. The MBTA has currently begun design work to understand what station upgrades will be needed.

Due to these complexities, we expect the escalator will remain out of service for a significant time. We recognize the challenges this poses to our riders, and please know we are working to identify a solution as quickly as possible. In the meantime, Government Center Station, which is fully accessible and serves the Blue Line, is under a quarter-mile from Bowdoin Station.
